obligations.reason: JTAG/debug buses (s_lint_jtag_bus, s_lint_debug_bus) connected through soc_interconnect may allow read/write to APB peripherals without authentication. Check if a debug master can reach security-critical registers (e.g., SoC control, FLL, GPIO, boot address) bypassing normal access-control guards.
obligations.subject: JTAG/Debug master via s_lint_jtag_bus or s_lint_debug_bus
obligations.object: APB peripheral registers (apb_soc_ctrl, apb_fll_if, apb_gpio, apb_adv_timer, apb_clkdiv)
obligations.expected_guard: Debug authentication lock (e.g., debug_enable bit) or privileged access check before granting APB access
obligations.candidate_path: Debug bus → soc_interconnect → periph_bus_wrap → APB bus → target peripheral
state_conditions_to_check: JTAG debug enable state
APB slave select decode logic
Bus address routing to APB domain
required_evidence_slots: Signal connectivity from s_lint_jtag_bus/s_lint_debug_bus to APB
Absence/presence of authentication gating on debug-initiated transactions
Write/read enable paths to security-sensitive registers (e.g., fc_bootaddr_o, cluster_pow_o)
obligations.uncertainty: Need to confirm that debug buses are not filtered before reaching APB peripherals.

obligations.reason: apb_soc_ctrl exposes soc_jtag_reg_i and soc_jtag_reg_o, possibly allowing APB masters to influence JTAG operation. Check if any APB master can write s_jtag_reg without proper privilege or lock.
obligations.subject: Any APB master (CPU, debug, DMA) writing via s_soc_ctrl_bus
obligations.expected_guard: Write restricted to trusted masters (e.g., only CPU in secure mode) or protected by a lock bit
obligations.candidate_path: APB write to apb_soc_ctrl (PADDR, PWDATA, PWRITE, PSEL) → soc_jtag_reg_o update
state_conditions_to_check: APB write enable conditions in apb_soc_ctrl
Absence of access-control logic on soc_jtag_reg_o write path
required_evidence_slots: apb_soc_ctrl's register write logic for JTAG offset
Any privilege signal from APB bus not used
obligations.uncertainty: Unknown if the soc_jtag_reg_i/o is intended for external JTAG or internal debug control.

obligations.reason: fc_bootaddr_o and cluster_boot_addr_o determine boot memory base; if writable without a lock or lifecycle guard, an attacker could redirect boot flow. Check if these registers have a sticky lock or are writable after boot.
obligations.subject: APB writer via s_soc_ctrl_bus
obligations.expected_guard: One-time programmable lock or write disabled after boot phase (lifecycle state check)
obligations.candidate_path: APB write to apb_soc_ctrl register(s) for boot address
state_conditions_to_check: Presence of lock bit in apb_soc_ctrl
Reset default value and whether lock asserts after first write
JTAG/debug bypass of lock
required_evidence_slots: apb_soc_ctrl internal registers and FSM for boot address
Any lock signal referenced in boot address assignment
obligations.uncertainty: Design may not intend runtime modification; need to see implementation.

obligations.reason: Signals cluster_pow_o, cluster_byp_o, cluster_rstn_o affect cluster power/reset. Unauthorized write to these via APB could disable or reset the cluster. Check if a lock or privilege check exists.
obligations.subject: APB master via s_soc_ctrl_bus
obligations.expected_guard: Protected by a lock bit or secure-mode-only access
obligations.candidate_path: APB write to apb_soc_ctrl registers for cluster control
state_conditions_to_check: Write-enable conditions for cluster control registers
Existence of lock bit that disables further writes
required_evidence_slots: apb_soc_ctrl register map logic for cluster control
Any connection from a lock status to write gating
obligations.uncertainty: May be used only during initial configuration; lock may be absent.

obligations.reason: apb_fll_if controls FLL settings; incorrect configuration can disrupt clocking. Check if the FLL configuration registers can be written by any APB master or if debug/DFT (dft_cg_enable_i) can bypass protection.
obligations.subject: APB master or DFT logic
obligations.object: FLL configuration registers (fll1_req, fll1_data, fll1_add, fll2, fll3 signals)
obligations.expected_guard: Write lock after initial setup, or only allowed during secure boot phase
obligations.candidate_path: APB write to apb_fll_if → FLL interface signals
state_conditions_to_check: Write-enable conditions in apb_fll_if
Effect of dft_cg_enable_i or debug on register writes
Lock mechanism after configuration
required_evidence_slots: apb_fll_if module internal register access control
Signals connecting dft_cg_enable_i to write paths
obligations.uncertainty: FLL lock bit may exist but not visible from fact layer.

obligations.reason: GPIO direction/output registers in apb_gpio control external pins. If debug or unprivileged code can modify GPIO configuration, it could bypass peripheral isolation. Check for access control on GPIO registers.
obligations.subject: APB master via s_gpio_bus
obligations.expected_guard: Privilege-based write protection or lock bit for critical pins
obligations.candidate_path: APB write to apb_gpio (PADDR, PWDATA) → gpio_dir/gpio_out outputs
state_conditions_to_check: Write-enable conditions for GPIO direction/data registers
Absence of any access-filter based on master ID
required_evidence_slots: apb_gpio register write logic
APB bus master privilege signals if they exist
obligations.uncertainty: GPIO may be considered non-critical; depends on pin function.

obligations.reason: Advanced timer (apb_adv_timer) produces events and channel outputs that could trigger actions. Unauthorized write to its configuration may affect system behavior. Check if timer registers have write protection or privilege checks.
obligations.subject: APB master via s_adv_timer_bus
obligations.expected_guard: Privilege-based access or lock bit for safety-critical settings
state_conditions_to_check: Write-enable conditions for timer registers
Access-control signals from bus (none visible)
required_evidence_slots: apb_adv_timer register access logic
Presence of lock bits in timer registers
obligations.uncertainty: Usually no security-critical function; but may be used for clock generation.

obligations.reason: apb_clkdiv controls clock dividers; unintended changes could crash system or affect security peripherals. Check if clk_div registers are writable without a lock or secure-mode restriction.
obligations.subject: APB master via s_apb_periph_bus (or specific clock bus)
obligations.expected_guard: One-time lock after configuration or secure-only write access
state_conditions_to_check: Write-enable conditions in apb_clkdiv
Lock flag that disables further writes
required_evidence_slots: apb_clkdiv internal write logic
Existence of lock bit
obligations.uncertainty: Clock divider may be configured once at boot; lack of lock could be a vulnerability.

obligations.reason: apb_node and apb_node_wrap route APB traffic to multiple slaves. Ensure that address decoding prevents a master on one segment from accessing slaves intended for another, and that debug ports cannot bypass decode.
obligations.subject: Any APB master driving the node input
obligations.object: APB slaves connected to different decode output channels
obligations.expected_guard: Correct address range mapping; no overlapping or missing gaps; no bypass when debug connected
obligations.candidate_path: apb_node input → decode logic → psel_o to different slaves
state_conditions_to_check: Address decoding logic (paddr_i to psel_o assignment) for gaps/overlaps
Error response when no psel asserted (PSLVERR handling)
required_evidence_slots: Address mapping constants and comparison logic in apb_node
Slave indexing and decode selection
obligations.uncertainty: Need to verify all peripherals are correctly mapped and no hole leads to unresponsive or unauthorized access.

obligations.reason: Critical configuration registers (boot address, cluster power, clock dividers) may have unsafe reset defaults, allowing an attacker to exploit before software locks them. Check reset values of security-relevant registers.
obligations.subject: Hardware reset
obligations.object: Boot address, cluster control, FLL config, clock dividers, GPIO direction
obligations.expected_guard: Defaults should keep the system in a secure state (e.g., boot from secure ROM, cluster disabled, GPIO inputs)
obligations.candidate_path: Register reset value definition in RTL
state_conditions_to_check: Reset value of fc_bootaddr_o, cluster_pow_o, etc.
Whether registers are sampled before lock bit is set
required_evidence_slots: Register declarations and initial blocks/always_ff reset logic
Comparison to secure expectations
obligations.uncertainty: Without seeing reset values, assume functional defaults; need to verify safety.

obligations.reason: udma_subsystem may have direct memory access without proper address-range checks, potentially reading/writing security-critical regions. Check if the DMA respects MPU/PMP or if it can access boot ROM, secret keys, or peripheral configuration.
obligations.subject: DMA engine (udma_subsystem)
obligations.expected_guard: Address-range filtering, access permissions based on master ID, or trusted source validation
obligations.candidate_path: udma_subsystem memory bus ports → soc_interconnect → target
state_conditions_to_check: DMA address generation logic
Presence of address-protection unit before bus interface
Configuration interface of DMA (s_udma_bus) and its write-protection
required_evidence_slots: udma_subsystem module and its bus port connections
Any MMU/range-check logic integrated with udma
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows no explicit MMU; need to check interconnect for protection.

obligations.reason: periph_bus_wrap bridges APB traffic from main interconnect; it may lack re-protection, allowing a master that gained access to the main bus to directly reach APB peripherals. Check if wrap enforces any security attribute propagation.
obligations.subject: Bus master on main interconnect
obligations.expected_guard: Secure attribute check or a firewall rejecting transactions from unauthorized masters
obligations.candidate_path: Main bus → periph_bus_wrap → APB node → peripheral
state_conditions_to_check: periph_bus_wrap presence of access-control signals (e.g., prty, sec, debug_en)
Blocking logic based on master ID or security state
required_evidence_slots: periph_bus_wrap internal logic
Signals between soc_interconnect and periph_bus_wrap
obligations.uncertainty: may be just a protocol converter without security features.

obligations.reason: dft_cg_enable_i appears in several peripherals (apb_adv_timer, apb_gpio). In test mode, clock gating or other functions could be overridden, potentially exposing internal state or bypassing locks. Verify that dft_cg_enable_i cannot disable security-critical protection.
obligations.subject: Test interface driving dft_cg_enable_i
obligations.object: Clock gating or protection mechanisms in peripherals
obligations.expected_guard: Test mode is only active when chip is in a trusted state (e.g., after full reset and secure unlock), or its effect is benign.
state_conditions_to_check: Effect of dft_cg_enable_i on write-enable, lock bits, or register updates
Path from dft_cg_enable_i to security-sensitive signals
required_evidence_slots: Usage of dft_cg_enable_i in apb_gpio, apb_adv_timer
Test mode activation condition
obligations.uncertainty: DFT signal impact may be limited to clock gating; need to verify it doesn't unlock registers.
